description: Cross-encoder relevance logits for (query, passage) pairs — Spike D parity oracle. Generated with the validated Spike D config (AutoModelForSequenceClassification, fp32) on the same ONNX weights the TS cross-encoder loads, which Spike D validated at max|diff|=0.0000 against cross-encoder/ms-marco-MiniLM-L-12-v2. Read-only oracle; never regenerate to pass a test.
query: How does HNSW vector search work?
passages: HNSW builds a navigable small-world graph with multiple layers so nearest-neighbour search runs in logarithmic time.
Cosine similarity measures the angle between two vectors as the dot product of their unit-normalized forms.
Bananas are an excellent dietary source of potassium and vitamin B6.
A hierarchical navigable small world index greedily descends layers, refining the candidate neighbour set at each step.
The capital city of France is Paris, situated on the river Seine.
